title: rhamnaceae
intro: The Rhamnaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the buckthorn or dogwood family. This family consists of approximately 700 species of deciduous and evergreen shrubs and small trees found in temperate regions around the world. Rhamnaceae members are characterized by their small, often inconspicuous flowers and berry-like fruits. They are commonly found in various ecosystems, including woodlands and rocky areas, and are often valued for their ornamental and medicinal properties.
